columns.name: LogonType
columns.description:
Type of logon session, specifically:

- **Interactive** - User physically interacts with the device using the local keyboard and screen

- **Remote interactive (RDP) logons** - User interacts with the device remotely using Remote Desktop, Terminal Services, Remote Assistance, or other RDP clients

- **Network** - Session initiated when the device is accessed using PsExec or when shared resources on the device, such as printers and shared folders, are accessed

- **Batch** - Session initiated by scheduled tasks

- **Service** - Session initiated by services as they start

columns.description: List of confidence levels for each threat type identified

columns.name: DetectionMethods
columns.description: Methods used to detect malware, phishing, or other threats found in the message

columns.name: LatestDeliveryLocation
columns.description: Last known location of the message

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the event

columns.name: IsExternalThread
columns.description: Indicates if there are external recipients in the thread (1) or none (0)

columns.name: SafetyTip
columns.description: The safety tip that has been added on a message, if any

columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: Date and time when the event was recorded

columns.name: TeamsMessageId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the message, as generated by Microsoft 365

columns.name: Url
columns.description: URL from message

columns.name: UrlDomain
columns.description: Domain name or host name of the URL

columns.name: ThreatTypes
columns.description: Verdict from the filtering stack on whether the message contains malware, phishing, or other threats

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the event

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: Unique identifier for the record

columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: Date and time when the record was created

columns.name: OAuthAppId
columns.description: The unique  identifier for the app as assigned by Microsoft Entra ID

columns.name: ServicePrincipalId
columns.description: The unique identifier for the service principal instance of the application in the tenant

columns.name: AppName
columns.description: The application's display name as exposed by the associated service principal

columns.name: AddedOnTime
columns.description: Date and time when the application was registered

columns.name: LastModifiedTime
columns.description: Timestamp when the app was last modified

columns.name: AppStatus
columns.description: Status of the app; can be: Enabled, DisabledByMicrosoft, DisabledByAppGovernancePolicy, DisabledByUser, Deleted (information for apps with Deleted status is only available for 30 days since the app was deleted)

columns.name: VerifiedPublisher
columns.description: Specifies details about the verified publisher of the application which this service principal represents. It includes information such as: DisplayName, VerifiedPublisherId, AddedDateTime

columns.name: PrivilegeLevel
columns.description: The privilege level of the app based on the highest classified permission granted to the app

columns.name: Permissions
columns.description: Contains an array of permission objects; each permission object includes PermissionName, TargetAppId, TargetAppDisplayName, PermissionType, PrivilegeLevel, UsageStatus

columns.name: ConsentedUsersCount
columns.description: Count of users who have consented to the app; this information is only available when the app isn't admin consented

columns.name: IsAdminConsented
columns.description: Value is True if a user has provided admin consent to the app on behalf of all the users in the org, otherwise the value is False

columns.name: AppOrigin
columns.description: Specifies whether the app is internal to the organization or registered in an external tenant

columns.name: LastUsedTime
columns.description: Date and time when the app last signed in. Tracking of this data goes back to June, 2022

columns.name: AppOwnerTenantId
columns.description: Specifies the ID of the tenant where the app was registered

columns.name: Timestamp
columns.description: The date and time when the user clicked on the link

columns.name: Url
columns.description: The full URL that was clicked on by the user

columns.name: ActionType
columns.description: Indicates whether the click was allowed or blocked by Safe Links or blocked due to a tenant policy, for instance, from Tenant Allow Block list

columns.name: AccountUpn
columns.description: User Principal Name of the account that clicked on the link

columns.name: Workload
columns.description: The application from which the user clicked on the link, with the values being Email, Office, and Teams

columns.name: NetworkMessageId
columns.description: The unique identifier for the email that contains the clicked link, generated by Microsoft 365

columns.name: ThreatTypes
columns.description: Verdict at the time of click, which tells whether the URL led to malware, phish or other threats

columns.name: DetectionMethods
columns.description: Detection technology that was used to identify the threat at the time of click

columns.name: IPAddress
columns.description: Public IP address of the device from which the user clicked on the link

columns.name: IsClickedThrough
columns.description: Indicates whether the user was able to click through to the original URL (1) or not (0) on the Safe Links warning page using "Continue anyway" option

columns.name: UrlChain
columns.description: For scenarios involving redirections, it includes URLs present in the redirection chain

columns.name: ReportId
columns.description: The unique identifier for a click event. For clickthrough scenarios, report ID would have same value, and therefore it should be used to correlate a click event.
